title: Read-aloud / low-literacy 'easy-read' explainer variants
context: The core explainer content, even when plain-language, may exceed the reading level of some target beneficiaries — particularly those with low literacy, cognitive disabilities, or who are reading in their second language. Easy-read variants use shorter sentences, simpler vocabulary, visual supports, and audio/read-aloud compatibility to extend access. All easy-read content must carry the same no-verdict and limitations framing as the standard explainers, and must be expert-reviewed for technical accuracy alongside a11y review.
objective: Author easy-read variants of the core explainer content (simplified vocabulary, shorter sentences, visual support cues, read-aloud optimized markup) for the main contaminant entries and the explainer template: (1) meets a low-literacy readability target (e.g. Flesch-Kincaid Grade <= 6); (2) carries no safety verdict or grade; (3) includes the standard limitations and 'not a substitute for official advisories' framing; (4) is expert-reviewed for technical accuracy; (5) is a11y reviewed for screen-reader and read-aloud compatibility; (6) licensed CC-BY-4.0.
acceptanceCriteria: Easy-read variants meet a low-literacy readability target (Flesch-Kincaid Grade <= 6 or equivalent)
Variants carry no safety verdict, grade, ranking, or product recommendation
Variants include the standard limitations section and 'not a substitute for official advisories' framing
Expert review completed for technical accuracy of health/regulatory content
Accessibility review completed for screen-reader and read-aloud compatibility
output: Document: easy-read explainer variants for core contaminant entries and the explainer template, with expert and a11y review records.
